context:
People in this club who perform in school talent shows often attend and are very engaged with school events.
People in this club either perform in school talent shows often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers and educational opportunities are students who attend the school. 
Bonnie is in this club and she either both attends and is very engaged with school events and is a student who attends the school or is not someone who both attends and is very engaged with school events and is not a student who attends the school.
conclusion: Bonnie performs in school talent shows often.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:

performs_in_talent_shows(x) ::: x performs in school talent shows often
attends_events(x) ::: x attends and is very engaged with school events
student(x) ::: x is a student who attends the school
inactive(x) ::: x is an inactive and disinterested community member
chaperones_dances(x) ::: x chaperones high school dances

PREMISES:
forall x (performs_in_talent_shows(x) -> attends_events(x))
forall x (performs_in_talent_shows(x) -> student(x))
forall x (inactive(x) -> chaperones_dances(x))
forall x (inactive(x) -> chaperones_dances(x))
forall x (student(x) -> attends_events(x))
performs_in_talent_shows(bonnie) V NOT (attends_events(bonnie) AND student(bonnie))
performs_in_talent_shows(bonnie)

CONCLUSION:
performs_in_talent_shows(bonnie)

People in this club who perform in school talent shows often attend and are very engaged with school events.
People in this club either perform in school talent shows often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers and educational opportunities are students who attend the school. 
Bonnie is in this club and she either both attends and is very engaged with school events and is a student who attends the school or is not someone who both attends and is very engaged with school events and is not a student who attends the school.
conclusion: If Bonnie is either both a young child or teenager who wishes to further her academic career and educational opportunities and chaperones high school dances or neither is a young child nor teenager who wishes to further her academic career and educational opportunities, then Bonnie is either a student who attends the school or is an inactive and disinterested member of the community.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
performs(x) ::: x performs in school talent shows
attends_events(x) ::: x is very engaged with school events
inactive(x) ::: x is an inactive and disinterested community member
chaperones(x) ::: x chaperones high school dances
student(x) ::: x is a student who attends the school
young_child(x) ::: x is a young child
teenager(x) ::: x is a teenager
wishes_academic(x) ::: x wishes to further her academic career and educational opportunities
club_member(x) ::: x is in this club

PREMISES:
forall x (club_member(x) -> (performs(x) \/ attends_events(x)))
forall x (club_member(x) -> (performs(x) -> attends_events(x)))
forall x (club_member(x) -> (chaperones(x) -> NOT student(x)))
forall x (club_member(x) -> (inactive(x) -> chaperones(x)))
forall x (club_member(x) -> (young_child(x) \/ teenager(x) \/ wishes_academic(x) -> student(x)))
club_member(bonnie)
(club_member(bonnie) -> (attends_events(bonnie) /\ student(bonnie) \/ NOT (attends_events(bonnie) /\ student(bonnie))))

CONCLUSION:
((young_child(bonnie) \/ teenager(bonnie) \/ wishes_academic(bonnie) /\ chaperones(bonnie) \/ NOT (young_child(bonnie) \/ teenager(bonnie) \/ wishes_academic(bonnie))) -> (student(bonnie) \/ inactive(bonnie)))

context:
All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today. 
Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers. 
Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home. 
No managers work remotely from home. 
James will appear in the company today if and only if he is a manager.
conclusion: James has lunch in the company.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
employee(x) ::: x is an employee
schedules_meeting_with_customers(x) ::: x schedules a meeting with their customers
goes_to_company_building_today(x) ::: x goes to the company building today
lunch_in_company_building(x) ::: x has lunch in the company building
lunch_at_home(x) ::: x has lunch at home
working_remotely_from_home(x) ::: x is working remotely from home
in_other_countries(x) ::: x is in other countries
manager(x) ::: x is a manager
appears_in_company_today(x) ::: x will appear in the company today

PREMISES:
forall x (employee(x) -> (schedules_meeting_with_customers(x) -> goes_to_company_building_today(x)))
forall x (employee(x) -> (lunch_in_company_building(x) -> schedules_meeting_with_customers(x)))
forall x (employee(x) -> (lunch_at_home(x) -> working_remotely_from_home(x)))
forall x (employee(x) -> (in_other_countries(x) -> working_remotely_from_home(x)))
forall x (employee(x) -> (working_remotely_from_home(x) -> NOT in_other_countries(x)))
forall x (employee(x) -> (working_remotely_from_home(x) -> NOT manager(x)))
employee(james) -> (appears_in_company_today(james) <-> manager(james))

CONCLUSION:
lunch_in_company_building(james)

context:
In superhero movies, the good guys always win.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is a superhero movie.
Good guys fight bad guys and vice versa.
Sir Digby fights his nemesis.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is a good guy.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is named after Sir Digby.
If somebody wins a fight, the person they are fighting does not win.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is in the movie.
conclusion: Sir Digby’s nemesis does not win.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
good_guy(x) ::: x is a good guy
bad_guy(x) ::: x is a bad guy
superhero_movie(x) ::: x is a superhero movie
win(x) ::: x wins
nemesis(x, y) ::: x fights y and y is x's nemesis
named_after(x, y) ::: x is named after y
in_movie(x, y) ::: x is in y

PREMISES:
forall x (superhero_movie(x) -> good_guy(x))
superhero_movie(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar)
forall x (superhero_movie(x) -> forall y (good_guy(y) \/ bad_guy(y)))
nemesis(sir_digby, his_nemesis)
forall x (superhero_movie(x) -> forall y (named_after(x, y) -> good_guy(y)))
named_after(the_surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar, sir_digby)
forall x (win(x) -> NOT win(y))
forall x (superhero_movie(x) -> forall y (named_after(x, y) -> in_movie(y, x)))

CONCLUSION:
NOT win(his_nemesis)

context:
Books contain tons of knowledge.
When a person reads a book, that person gains knowledge. 
If a person gains knowledge, they become smarter.
Harry read the book “Walden” by Henry Thoreau.
conclusion: Walden contains knowledge.

book(x) ::: x is a book
contains(x,y) ::: x contains y
knowledge(x) ::: x is knowledge
read(x,y) ::: x read y
person(x) ::: x is a person
smarter(x) ::: x is smarter
title(x,y) ::: x is titled y

forall x (book(x) -> contains(x,knowledge))
forall x (person(x) -> forall y (read(x,y) -> gains(x,knowledge)))
forall x (gains(x,knowledge) -> smarter(x))
book(book_walden)
person(harry)
read(harry,book_walden)

CONCLUSION:
knowledge(book_walden)

PREDICATES:

forall x (book(x) -> contains(x))
forall x (reads(x, book(y)) -> gainsKnowledge(x, y))

reads(harry, walden)

context:
A natural language processing task can be categorized as either a language generation task or a language understanding task.
If the output of a natural language processing task is a text sequence, then it is a language generation task.
Machine translation is a natural language processing task.
The output of machine translation is a text sequence.
conclusion: Machine translation is a language generation task.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
natural_language_processing_task(x) ::: x is a natural language processing task
language_generation_task(x) ::: x is a language generation task
language_understanding_task(x) ::: x is a language understanding task
output_text_sequence(x) ::: the output of x is a text sequence

PREMISES:
forall x (natural_language_processing_task(x) -> (language_generation_task(x) | language_understanding_task(x)))
forall x (output_text_sequence(x) -> language_generation_task(x))
natural_language_processing_task(machine_translation)
output_text_sequence(machine_translation)

CONCLUSION:
language_generation_task(machine_translation)

context:
Pets are allowed in some managed buildings.
A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more.
Fluffy is Tom's cat.
Cats are pets.
The Olive Garden is a managed building.
The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
$2000 is more than $1500.
Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy, and the security deposit is no more than $1500.
If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
conclusion: Tom will rent an apartment in The Olive Garden.

monthly_rent(x, y) ::: x is the monthly rent for y
equal_to(x, y) ::: x is equal to y
more_than(x, y) ::: x is more than y
cat(x) ::: x is a cat
is_equal_to(x, y) ::: x is equal to y
is_more_than(x, y) ::: x is more than y

forall x (requires_deposit(managed_building(x)) -> (security_deposit(x, y) -> (equal_to(security_deposit(x, y), monthly_rent(x, y)) | more_than(security_deposit(x, y), monthly_rent(x, y)))))
pet(fluffy)
cat(fluffy)
managed_building(olive_garden)
monthly_rent(olive_garden, 2000)
is_equal_to(2000, 2000)

rents_apartment(tom, olive_garden)

context:
Pets are allowed in some managed buildings.
A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more.
Fluffy is Tom's cat.
Cats are pets.
The Olive Garden is a managed building.
The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
$2000 is more than $1500.
Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy, and the security deposit is no more than $1500.
If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
conclusion: The security deposit at the Olive Garden is either $2000 or more.

If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.

context:

All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
Peter is either a person who spills a lot of food on his clothing and is cleanly, or a person who does not spill a lot of food on his clothing and is not cleanly
conclusion: Peter is either both a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try and issomeone whose family prioritizes order and spotlessness, or he neither is a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try nor does his family prioritize order and spotlessness.

context:
All people who make wedding plans are people who are engaged.
All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.
Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans.
If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
conclusion: John has a larger family.
label: False
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
engaged(x) ::: x is engaged
makes_wedding_plans(x) ::: x makes wedding plans
invites_others(x) ::: x invites others to their ceremony
well_attended_wedding(x) ::: x has a well-attended wedding
larger_family(x) ::: x has a larger family

PREMISES:
forall x (makes_wedding_plans(x) -> engaged(x))
forall x (invites_others(x) -> makes_wedding_plans(x))
forall x (well_attended_wedding(x) -> invites_others(x))
exists x (well_attended_wedding(x) & larger_family(x))
engaged(john) -> NOT (invites_others(john) & makes_wedding_plans(john))
larger_family(john) -> (well_attended_wedding(john) | invites_others(john))

CONCLUSION:
larger_family(john)

context:
Machine Learning algorithms can be categorized as supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.
Unsupervised learning algorithms do not require labeled data.
The state-of-the-art text summarization model is trained with machine learning algorithms.
Reinforcement learning is not used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model.
The Machine Learning algorithm for training text summarization models requires labeled data.
conclusion: Supervised learning is used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
machine_learning_algorithm(x) ::: x is a machine learning algorithm
supervised_learning(x) ::: x is a supervised learning algorithm
unsupervised_learning(x) ::: x is an unsupervised learning algorithm
reinforcement_learning(x) ::: x is a reinforcement learning algorithm
requires_labeled_data(x) ::: x requires labeled data
trained_with(x, y) ::: x is trained with y
state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model() ::: the state-of-the-art text summarization model

PREMISES:
forall x (machine_learning_algorithm(x) -> (supervised_learning(x) | unsupervised_learning(x) | reinforcement_learning(x)))
forall x (unsupervised_learning(x) -> NOT requires_labeled_data(x))
machine_learning_algorithm(state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model())
NOT reinforcement_learning(state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model())
requires_labeled_data(state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model())

CONCLUSION:
supervised_learning(state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model())

machine_learning_algorithm(x) ::: x is a machine learning algorithm
supervised_learning(x) ::: x is a supervised learning algorithm
unsupervised_learning(x) ::: x is an unsupervised learning algorithm
reinforcement_learning(x) ::: x is a reinforcement learning algorithm
requires_labeled_data(x) ::: x requires labeled data
trained_with(x, y) ::: x is trained with y
state_of_the_art_text_summarization_model() ::: the state-of-the-art text summarization model

PREMISES:

A person either has United States citizenship or Taiwanese citizenship.
No Russian Federation officials hold Taiwanese citizenship. 
Vladimir neither holds Taiwanese citizenship nor is he a manager at Gazprom. 
Ekaterina she can register to vote in the United States, or she is a Russian federation official.
conclusion: Ekaterina can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election or is a manager at Gazprom.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
can_register_us(x) ::: x can register to vote in the United States
can_participate_2024(x) ::: x can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election
has_us_citizenship(x) ::: x has United States citizenship
has_taiwanese_citizenship(x) ::: x has Taiwanese citizenship
is_russian_official(x) ::: x is a Russian Federation official
is_manager_gazprom(x) ::: x is a manager at Gazprom

PREMISES:
forall x (can_register_us(x) -> can_participate_2024(x))
forall x (has_us_citizenship(x) -> can_register_us(x))
forall x (has_us_citizenship(x) V has_taiwanese_citizenship(x))
forall x (is_russian_official(x) -> NOT has_taiwanese_citizenship(x))
NOT has_taiwanese_citizenship(vladimir) V NOT is_manager_gazprom(vladimir)
can_register_us(ekaterina) V is_russian_official(ekaterina)

CONCLUSION:
can_participate_2024(ekaterina) V is_manager_gazprom(ekaterina)

context:
New Vessel Press is a publishing house specializing in translating foreign literature into English.
All of New Vessel Press's published books are in English.
Neapolitan Chronicles is a book published by New Vessel Press.
Neapolitan Chronicles was translated from Italian.
Palace of Flies is a book published by New Vessel Press.
conclusion: Neapolitan Chronicles is an English book.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
publishes(x, y) ::: x publishes y
specializes_in(x, y) ::: x specializes in y
in_english(x) ::: x is in English
translated_from(x, y) ::: x was translated from y

PREMISES:
forall x (publishes(New Vessel Press, x) -> in_english(x))
publishes(New Vessel Press, Neapolitan Chronicles)
publishes(New Vessel Press, Palace of Flies)
specializes_in(New Vessel Press, translating foreign literature into English)
translated_from(Neapolitan Chronicles, Italian)

CONCLUSION:
in_english(Neapolitan Chronicles)

publishing_house(x) ::: x is a publishing house

translated_from(x, y) ::: x was translated from y

specializing_in_translating(new_vessel_press, foreign_literature)
forall x (published_by(new_vessel_press, x) -> in_english(x))
published_by(new_vessel_press, neapolitan Chronicles)
translated_from(neapolitan Chronicles, italian)

CONCLUSION:
in_english(neapolitan Chronicles)

All organic compounds are chemical compounds.
All organic compounds contain carbon.
No chemical compounds contain only one element.
The mixture either both is a chemical compound and contains only one element, or neither.
conclusion: If the mixture contains only one element or contains carbon, then the mixture is neither a chemical compound nor an alkane.
label: True
formalisations:

forall x (organic_compound(x) -> contains_carbon(x))
forall x (chemical_compound(x) -> NOT contains_only_one_element(x))
forall x ((contains_only_one_element(x) AND chemical_compound(x)) OR (NOT contains_only_one_element(x) AND NOT chemical_compound(x)))

CONCLUSION:
contains_only_one_element(mixture) OR contains_carbon(mixture) -> NOT chemical_compound(mixture) AND NOT alkane(mixture)

If people visit the gym at least once a day, then they always fulfill their daily nutritional intakes.
Taylor either grew up in a health-conscious childhood home and disregard her physical well-being, or she did neither.
conclusion: Taylor neither grew up in a health-conscious childhood home nor does she visit the gym at least once a day.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
eats_salads_regularly(x) ::: x eats salads regularly
very_conscious_about_health_and_eating_habits(x) ::: x is very conscious about health and eating habits
grew_up_in_health_conscious_home(x) ::: x grew up in a health-conscious childhood home
fulfills_daily_nutritional_intakes(x) ::: x fulfills daily nutritional intakes
disregards_physical_well_being(x) ::: x disregards physical well-being
visits_gym_at_least_once_a_day(x) ::: x visits the gym at least once a day

PREMISES:
forall x (eats_salads_regularly(x) -> very_conscious_about_health_and_eating_habits(x))
forall x (grew_up_in_health_conscious_home(x) -> eats_salads_regularly(x))
forall x (fulfills_daily_nutritional_intakes(x) -> grew_up_in_health_conscious_home(x))
forall x (disregards_physical_well_being(x) -> NOT very_conscious_about_health_and_eating_habits(x))
forall x (visits_gym_at_least_once_a_day(x) -> fulfills_daily_nutritional_intakes(x))
grew_up_in_health_conscious_home(taylor) OR NOT visits_gym_at_least_once_a_day(taylor)

CONCLUSION:
NOT grew_up_in_health_conscious_home(taylor) AND NOT visits_gym_at_least_once_a_day(taylor)

context:
A podcast is not a novel.
If a person is born in American City, the person is American.
If a book is a novel and it is written by a person, then the person is a novel writer.
Dani Shapiro is an American writer.
Family History is written by Dani Shapiro.
Family History is a novel written in 2003.
Dani Shapiro created a podcast called Family Secrets.
Boston is an American city.
conclusion: Dani Shapiro is a novel writer.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
podcast(x) ::: x is a podcast
novel(x) ::: x is a novel
born_in(x, y) ::: x is born in y
american(x) ::: x is american
written_by(x, y) ::: x is written by y
novel_writer(x) ::: x is a novel writer
created(x, y) ::: x created y
city(x) ::: x is a city
american_city(x) ::: x is an american city

PREMISES:
forall x (NOT podcast(x) -> novel(x))
forall x y (born_in(x, y) -> american(x))
forall x y z (novel(x) & written_by(x, y) -> novel_writer(y))
american(dani_shapiro)
novel(family_history)
written_by(family_history, dani_shapiro)
podcast(family_secrets)
created(dani_shapiro, family_secrets)
city(boston)
american_city(american_city)

CONCLUSION:
novel_writer(dani_shapiro)
